expectations.text: Adds kelet to pyproject.toml or requirements
expectations.evidence: Transcript notes kelet>=1.3.0 was already in pyproject.toml; skill correctly detected and skipped install step. Dependency satisfied.

expectations.text: kelet.configure() is called at startup (not per-request)
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `kelet.configure()` added at module level in app/main.py (line 19), outside any request handler.

expectations.text: agentic_session() wraps the agent call with the existing Redis session_id
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `async with kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id):` wrapping `chat_agent.iter()` in src/routers/chat.py.

expectations.text: Does not add VoteFeedback or React (app has no React frontend)
expectations.evidence: Transcript explicitly notes 'No React frontend → no VoteFeedback, no useFeedbackState'. Signal analysis confirms 0 coded signals. No React or VoteFeedback references in diff.

expectations.text: KELET_API_KEY and KELET_PROJECT written to .env (not hardcoded in source)
expectations.evidence: Transcript lines 164-166 confirm .env updated with KELET_PROJECT=docs-ai-assistant and KELET_API_KEY=sk-kelet-test-123. Keys not hardcoded in source.

expectations.text: AskUserQuestion called at most 3 times total
expectations.evidence: questions_asked.md shows exactly 2 AskUserQuestion calls (Checkpoint 1 and Checkpoint 2). Well within the 3-max budget.

expectations.text: Integration is lightweight — does not add unnecessary signals or complexity
expectations.evidence: Transcript: 'Coded signals: 0 (lightweight, no hooks to wire to)'. Changes limited to configure(), agentic_session(), and env vars.

expectations.text: Synthetic evaluator deeplink is generated via Bash execution (not shown as copy-paste code block)
expectations.evidence: deeplink.txt contains a real https://console.kelet.ai/docs-ai-assistant/synthetics/setup?deeplink=<base64url> URL.

expectations.evidence: session_handling.md documents the mismatch detection in detail. Transcript states 'Mismatch flagged: phone outlives sessions; should be user_id= not session_id'.

expectations.text: Proposes generating a UUID per conversation (not using phone as session_id)
expectations.evidence: session_handling.md confirms the app already generates UUIDs correctly via create_session(). The fix proposed is to keep UUID as session_id and add phone_number as user_id.

expectations.text: Passes stable identifier (phone) as user_id= in agentic_session()
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id, user_id=user_id)` with `phone_number: str | None = None` added to ChatRequest.

expectations.text: agentic_session() wraps agent call with session_id
expectations.evidence: changes.diff NOTE confirms kelet.agentic_session() was already in place wrapping the agent stream. Transcript confirms 'kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id) wrapping chat_agent.iter() — complete: session context spans the entire streaming generator'.

expectations.text: KeletProvider added at React root with publishable key
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows KeletProvider imported and added in frontend/src/main.tsx with apiKey={import.meta.env.VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY} and project={import.meta.env.VITE_KELET_PROJECT}, wrapping App inside React.StrictMode.

expectations.text: VoteFeedback component added next to AI message output (not a floating widget or separate page)
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows VoteFeedback.Root with UpvoteButton (asChild), DownvoteButton (asChild), Popover, Textarea, and SubmitButton inside AssistantMessage component — inline next to each assistant message, not a floating widget.

expectations.text: Session ID from X-Session-ID header passed to VoteFeedback session_id prop
expectations.evidence: frontend_changes.md documents the full end-to-end flow: server returns X-Session-ID header, React captures via res.headers.get('X-Session-ID') stored as sessionId state, then VoteFeedback.Root session_id={sessionId}.

expectations.text: Both KELET_API_KEY and VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY present in .env (pre-existing or added)?
expectations.evidence: changes.diff NOTE confirms '.env already contained KELET_API_KEY, KELET_PROJECT, VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY, and VITE_KELET_PROJECT'. Both keys present — assertion allows pre-existing keys.

expectations.text: VoteFeedback styled to match dark theme using existing CSS classes rather than arbitrary inline styles or emoji
expectations.evidence: Vote buttons use asChild pattern with className={styles.iconBtn} (existing CSS module class). SubmitButton uses className={styles.sendBtn} (same class as the main Send button). Popover inline styles precisely match app's dark-theme palette (#1e2130, #2d3748 — same values in App.module.css). No emoji used anywhere.

expectations.text: Does transcript/frontend_changes.md propose useFeedbackState or useKeletSignal for at least one interaction beyond VoteFeedback?
expectations.evidence: Transcript signal analysis states 'No copy button, no retry, no edit-in-place, no session reset button' and concludes 'VoteFeedback is the only viable coded signal'. No useFeedbackState or useKeletSignal was proposed. Skill stopped at VoteFeedback without surfacing additional signal opportunities.

notes: Iteration 3: on_brand_ui fix — skill now instructs matching app visual style for all UI additions
Eval 4 with_skill re-run (iteration 3) fixes on_brand_ui: now uses styles.iconBtn + styles.sendBtn for vote buttons and submit, matching app's existing CSS module classes

Eval 4 with_skill still fails implicit_fe_signals: skill applied lightweight mode correctly but did not surface additional signal opportunities (e.g. useKeletSignal for copy/retry) when React frontend is present
both_keys_in_env now passes in iter-3 (pre-existing keys count): KELET_API_KEY + VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY both present in .env at end of run
Session boundary detection shows largest gap (+75pp on eval 2): without skill, phone-as-user-id was silently dropped
Deeplink generation is 0% discoverable without the skill (0/3 non-trivial evals generated it)
Eval 4 React/UI integration shows massive gap (+67pp): without skill, wrong context manager, no KeletProvider, no VoteFeedback, no publishable key knowledge
Eval 5 expand-to-full shows modest gap (+20pp): without skill, signal instrumentation is largely achievable but synthetic evaluator workflow is completely unknown
